title: Binder projection durability — reconstruction from EventStore

all_binderservice_usages.note: export class Service extends Context.Service<Service, Interface>()("@opencode/BinderService") {}
import { Service as BinderService, type Binder as CanonicalBinder, layer as BinderLayer, BinderError, type LaneState as BinderLaneState } from "./binder"
section="transition" — adds transition events as evidence
section="event" — adds the triggering event as evidence
const binderOpt = yield* binderService.getBinder(laneId) — used to assemble LaneAuditData

recordBinderEvent.full_content:
function recordBinderEvent(
  eventStore: EventStore.Interface,
  eventType: string,
  laneId: string,
  payload: Record<string, unknown>,
): Effect.Effect<void> {
  return Effect.gen(function* () {
    yield* eventStore.record({
      id: Effect.runSync(Identifier.ascending("event")),
      sessionId: laneId,
      runId: laneId,
      ts: new Date().toISOString(),
      actor: "system" as const,
      eventType,
      phase: "campaign",
      toolName: "binder",
      filePath: undefined,
      model: undefined,
      durationMs: undefined,
      tokenInput: undefined,
      tokenOutput: undefined,
      errorCode: undefined,
      errorMessage: undefined,
      recoverable: undefined,
      parentEventId: undefined,
      correlationId: undefined,
      status: undefined,
      payloadJson: payload,
    })
  })
}

call_sites.note: BARE — only laneId + campaignId, no binder fields. Insufficient for reconstruction.
BARE — only laneId + section name. Cannot reconstruct which artifact was added.
STATUS ONLY — has new state but no prior state snapshot.
FINAL — has digest but no full binder snapshot.

recordBinderEvent.reconstruction_gap: ALL payloads are sparse. binder.created omits mission/scope. binder.evidence_added omits the artifact itself. binder.status_changed omits the previous status. binder.finalized omits the full binder state. None of the events carry enough payload to reconstruct a Binder object deterministically.

binder_interface.full_content:
export interface Binder {
  schemaVersion: string
  binderVersion: number
  laneId: string
  campaignId: string
  status: LaneState

  // Mission
  missionObjective: string
  laneScope: string
  claimedFiles: string[]
  dependencyLaneIds: string[]

  // Evidence chain
  scoutReports: ArtifactRef[]
  architecturePlan?: ArtifactRef
  criticReviews: ArtifactRef[]
  approvedPlan?: ArtifactRef

  // Execution
  executionEvents: EventRef[]
  diffSummary?: DiffSummary

  // Validation
  validationResults: ValidationResult[]
  redTeamFindings: RedTeamFinding[]
  repairHistory: RepairCycle[]

  // Closure
  checkpointCommit?: string
  residualRisks: string[]
  handoffSummary?: string
  terminalStatus?: TerminalStatus

  // Metadata
  createdAt: string
  completedAt?: string
  artifactDigest: string
}

binder_interface_service.full_content:
export interface Interface {
  readonly createBinder: (
    laneId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    mission: string,
    scope: string,
  ) => Effect.Effect<string>

  readonly getBinder: (laneId: string) => Effect.Effect<Option.Option<Binder>>

  readonly getBindersByCampaignId: (campaignId: string) => Effect.Effect<Binder[]>

  readonly addEvidence: (
    laneId: string,
    section: string,
    artifact: ArtifactRef | EventRef,
  ) => Effect.Effect<void, BinderError>

  readonly updateStatus: (
    laneId: string,
    status: LaneState,
    terminalStatus?: TerminalStatus,
  ) => Effect.Effect<void, BinderError>

  readonly finalizeBinder: (laneId: string) => Effect.Effect<Binder, BinderError>

  readonly getBinderDigest: (laneId: string) => Effect.Effect<string, BinderError>
}

query_filters.content:
export interface QueryFilters {
  sessionId?: string
  runId?: string
  eventType?: string
  actor?: string
  status?: string
  parentEventId?: string
  fromTs?: string
  toTs?: string
  limit?: number
  offset?: number
  order?: "asc" | "desc"
}

event_store.note: QueryFilters does NOT support filtering by toolName or laneId directly. To query binder events, you must filter by eventType (e.g. "binder.created") or use runId (which in binder events equals laneId). The buildWhere function only checks sessionId, runId, eventType, actor, status, parentEventId, fromTs, toTs.

secretary_init_replay.description: The secretary's init() function already demonstrates event replay from EventStore. It queries by runId=laneId, filters by payloadJson._tag, converts stored events to SMRuntimeEvent, and replays through reduceCampaignState. This is the closest analog for binder reconstruction.

explanation_engine.description: The explanation engine traces the EventStore ledger to reconstruct WHY something happened, querying by parentEventId and traversing the event tree. Uses in-memory caching for repeated lookups.

existing_tests.binder_tests: NONE — no test files matching *binder* in packages/opencode/test/
existing_tests.event_store_tests: NONE — no test files matching *event-store* or *event_store* in packages/opencode/test/
existing_tests.related_tests: No references to binder, event-store, or event_store found in any test file under packages/opencode/test/

binder_layer_provisioning.note: BinderService depends on EventStore for event persistence. Secretary's layer provides both BinderLayer and EventStore's layer.

smoking_guns.title: recordBinderEvent payloads are too sparse for reconstruction
smoking_guns.description: All four event types write minimal payloads — laneId only or laneId+status — missing the binder data that was actually created/modified. A reconstructBinder function replaying these events would produce an empty shell, not a useful Binder object.
evidence: binder.created (line 284): payload is { laneId, campaignId } — missing missionObjective, laneScope, full binder
binder.evidence_added (line 370): payload is { laneId, section } — missing the actual artifact that was added
binder.status_changed (line 399): payload is { laneId, status, terminalStatus } — has new state but not the full binder
binder.finalized (line 424): payload is { laneId, digest } — has digest but not the full binder object

smoking_guns.title: getBinder reads from in-memory Ref only — no fallback to EventStore
smoking_guns.description: The getBinder function (line 292) only does Ref.get(store). If the process restarted, the Ref is empty and getBinder returns Option.none. It never queries EventStore.
evidence: binder.ts:292-298: getBinder reads Ref only, returns Option.none if not found
binder.ts:299-303: getBindersByCampaignId also reads Ref only
binder.ts:432-440: getBinderDigest reads Ref only, fails with BinderError if not found
smoking_guns.impact: After restart, auditor.ts:431 and any other getBinder() consumer see no binder data even though events are persisted.

smoking_guns.title: EventStore QueryFilters lacks toolName filter — must query by eventType prefix or use runId
smoking_guns.description: The EventStore's QueryFilters interface has no toolName field and no laneId field. Binder events set toolName="binder" and sessionId=runId=laneId. To query binder events, you must filter by eventType (matching "binder.*" prefix) or filter by runId=laneId (which conflates session/run/lane IDs).
evidence: event-store.ts:11-24: QueryFilters has sessionId, runId, eventType, actor, status, parentEventId, fromTs, toTs, limit, offset, order — no toolName, no laneId
event-store.ts:90-101: buildWhere only checks sessionId, runId, eventType, actor, status, parentEventId, fromTs, toTs

smoking_guns.title: No existing tests for binder or event-store
smoking_guns.description: There are zero test files covering either binder.ts or event-store.ts. Any reconstruction logic will be added to untested code.
evidence: No files matching *event-store* or *event_store* in packages/opencode/test/
No references to binder or event-store in any test file

smoking_guns.title: duplicate-addEvidence hazard: addEvidence has idempotency risk
smoking_guns.description: addEvidence appends to arrays (scoutReports, criticReviews, etc.) with no duplicate detection. If a reconstructed binder replays events that were also added during the same lifetime, evidence sections will double. The reconstruction needs either an idempotency guard (eventId tracking) or a different replay strategy (full snapshot on finalize).
evidence: binder.ts:319-348: all switch cases use spread [...] append — no duplicate check
binder.ts:370: recordBinderEvent does not store the artifact in the event payload — even event replay cannot dedupe

smoking_guns.title: Reconstruction strategy precedent exists in secretary init
smoking_guns.description: The secretary's init() function (secretary.ts:306-353) already demonstrates the Exact Right Pattern: query EventStore by runId=laneId in ascending ts order, filter/transform events by payloadJson content, and replay through a reducer. This is the blueprint for reconstructBinder — but the payloads need to carry enough data first.
evidence: secretary.ts:314-317: eventStore.query({ runId: laneId, order: 'asc', limit: 10_000 })
secretary.ts:329-332: se.payloadJson && typeof se.payloadJson === 'object' && '_tag' in se.payloadJson → reconstruction filter
secretary.ts:337-338: reduceCampaignState(lane.smState, smEvents, LANE_STATE_MACHINE) — reducer replay pattern

design_constraints.title: ToolName in event-store does NOT map 1:1 to binder events uniquely
design_constraints.description: EventStore's buildWhere cannot filter by toolName. The only way to isolate binder events is eventType prefix match ("binder.*") or runId=laneId. Since sessionId=runId=laneId in binder events, filtering by runId works but pulls in all events for that laneId, not just binder events.

design_constraints.title: Binder is large — full-snapshot-on-every-event would be expensive
design_constraints.description: Binder contains arrays (scoutReports, criticReviews, executionEvents, validationResults, redTeamFindings, repairHistory) that grow over time. Writing the full Binder as payloadJson on every event would be wasteful. A better strategy: sparse delta events (like now) PLUS a full snapshot on binder.finalized.

design_constraints.title: Reconstruction needs ordered event replay — asc by ts
design_constraints.description: Since evidence is appended to arrays, replay must be in insertion order (ascending ts). The EventStore query supports order: 'asc', which matches the secretary init pattern.
